task_completion.description: Navigate to the Library of Congress website and search for WPA Travel or National Parks posters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window showing loc.gov with search results or collection pages for WPA posters related to Travel or National Parks.

task_completion.description: Create the target directory and save three distinct JPEG files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal showing the directory '~/Pictures/WPA_Travel/' exists and contains at least three distinct .jpg or .jpeg files.

task_completion.description: Ensure the downloaded posters are distinct Travel/National Park WPA posters and are the highest resolution JPEGs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Image viewer or file manager thumbnails showing three different WPA posters (e.g., 'See America', 'Yellowstone'). File sizes should be visible and typically around 145 KB or larger, indicating the larger JPEG option was downloaded rather than the smaller ~31 KB medium option.

task_completion.description: Generate a manifest.json file in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal showing a file named 'manifest.json' located exactly in '~/Pictures/WPA_Travel/'.

task_completion.description: The manifest.json contains an array of objects with the required keys for the downloaded items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor or terminal output displaying the contents of 'manifest.json'. It must be a valid JSON array containing three objects. Each object must contain exactly the keys: 'title', 'year', 'url', and 'filename'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the real Library of Congress website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar must show 'loc.gov' URLs, not a local file path or a mock website.

integrity.description: Files were actually downloaded from the website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser download history, active download popups, or 'Save As' dialogs should be visible in the trajectory, rather than files magically appearing via background scripts.

integrity.description: The manifest accurately reflects the downloaded files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'filename' values in the manifest.json must match the actual names of the JPEG files saved in the ~/Pictures/WPA_Travel/ directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Library of Congress (loc.gov) WPA poster collection dates exclusively from 1936-1943, which satisfies the task's '1930s-1950s' requirement. Valid posters include 'See America', 'Yellowstone National Park', 'Grand Canyon', etc. High-resolution JPEGs are typically around 145 KB (well above the 50 KB minimum), while medium JPEGs are around 31 KB. The agent must download the larger JPEG option. Both JPEG and TIFF formats are available on the site, but the task strictly requires JPEGs.
